title: Remove resize race condition
body:
Since podman-remote resize requests can come in at random times, this
generates a real potential for race conditions. We should only be
attempting to resize TTY on running containers, but the containers can
go from running to stopped at any time, and returning an error to the
caller is just causing noice.

This change will basically ignore requests to resize terminals if the
container is not running and return the caller to success.  All other
callers will still return failure.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9831

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@edsantiago PTAL, this is going to fail because of missing tests, do you have a handy test for this?
@mheon WDYT of this?

@rhatdan yes I do, and it's my favorite kind of test, the one that consists entirely of deletions!
```diff
diff --git a/test/system/450-interactive.bats b/test/system/450-interactive.bats
index d047b9f25..a2db39492 100644
--- a/test/system/450-interactive.bats
+++ b/test/system/450-interactive.bats
@@ -56,11 +56,7 @@ function teardown() {
     stty rows $rows cols $cols <$PODMAN_TEST_PTY

     # ...and make sure stty under podman reads that.
-    # FIXME: 'sleep 1' is needed for podman-remote; without it, there's
-    # a race condition resulting in the following warning:
-    #   WARN[0000] failed to resize TTY: container "xx" in wrong state "stopped"
-    # (also "created")
-    run_podman run -it --name mystty $IMAGE sh -c 'sleep 1;stty size' <$PODMAN_TEST_PTY
+    run_podman run -it --name mystty $IMAGE stty size <$PODMAN_TEST_PTY
     is "$output" "$rows $cols" "stty under podman reads the correct dimensions"
 }

```
Unfortunately, it is now triggering something else:
```
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: stty under podman reads the correct dimensions
   #| expected: '63 30'
'  #|   actual: 'stty: standard input
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```
This is a flake, about 50% of the time it passes (root and rootless), the rest it fails, always the same message. Before I try tracking it down further: does that message ("stty: standard input") mean anything to you? (He says, hoping there's an obvious quick fix)

The `stty: standard input` message does not happen if I leave the `sleep` in. (Or at least it doesn't happen over many retries). If you can't figure out the cause, just leave the `sleep` in place and change the comment:
```bash
# FIXME: 'sleep 1' is needed for podman-remote; without it, there's
# a race condition resulting in 'stty: standard input` instead of dimensions.
```
I can file an issue later. 

(Point being, by changing the comment, (a) you don't need NO TESTS NEEDED, (b) the comment is closer to reality, therefore helping a future developer)
My best guess is that it means the terminal never had a size assigned to it - but that's just a guess.

Hmmm I'm still seeing the stty failure with 2d525684185a5c2a2352b275db9b8e23444e5087. There are three podman-remote system tests, so I'm guessing one will pass but I do hope that one or two fail. I don't think this can go in as it is, without the stty error fixed.

/lgtm

because it's causing too many CI flakes
